notes: SHIP v1.14.349 - SAVER RENAME CLEANUP (pure mechanical, ZERO behavior change). Owner: 'yes clean them up' 2026-07-23, completing the .348 rename across the remaining three deployment savers. WHY: .347 (operator wipe) and .348 (assign-rack destroyed other deployments' racks) had the SAME root cause - reaching for an accessor whose SCOPE did not match the write's scope. In .348 the concrete trap was deploy_loadRacks(deployId) returning a FILTERED subset while deploy_saveRacks wrote its argument as the WHOLE key. .348 renamed that one saver to deploy_saveAllRacks so the asymmetry is visible at the call site; the other three kept the ambiguous name, which is the same trap still armed for the next call site somebody writes. CHANGE: deploy_savePhases -> deploy_saveAllPhases (8 sites), deploy_saveOptics -> deploy_saveAllOptics (4 sites), deploy_saveAudit -> deploy_saveAllAudit (3 sites). 15 sites total, each = 1 definition + its call sites; occurrence counts verified identical before and after; ZERO residue of the three old identifiers. All four deployment savers now read deploy_saveAll* and every one of them says at the call site that it writes the ENTIRE key. No function body, argument, order of operations, storage key, guard or toast changed - the ONLY edits are the identifier and the three version stamps. No CSS, no markup, no rd-gating, ?legacy=1 byte-identical by construction. REPORTED, NOT CHANGED (the other half of the asymmetry, needs an owner ruling): the LOADERS silently change SHAPE based on arity - deploy_loadRacks(deployId) returns one deployment's racks, deploy_loadRacks() returns every deployment's. That arity-dependent shape is what actually caused .348, and renaming only the savers does not remove it. Current usage: deploy_loadRacks 44 refs / 0 no-arg, deploy_loadOptics 19 / 0, deploy_loadAudit 14 / 0, deploy_loadPhases 22 refs / 4 no-arg - so the unscoped form is used in exactly 4 places, all of them checklist/phase MUTATION paths that legitimately need the whole array. A follow-up could split each loader into an explicit pair (deploy_loadRacksFor(deployId) + deploy_loadAllRacks()) and delete the arity branch entirely, which would make the .348 class of bug unrepresentable rather than merely visible. That is a ~99-site mechanical change across four loaders and is NOT in this ship. Gates: inline-JS vm.Script x3, CSS brace net-0 (no CSS touched), three-stamp .348->.349, rename residue check on all three old names, occurrence-count parity, all three prior offline suites (.346 25/25, .347 9/9, .348 10/10) re-run green against the renamed file. DEVICE-VERIFY (clear SW first, low-risk regression sweep only): open a deployment, advance a phase, block a phase and add a blocker note, tick a phase-checklist item, dispense/install an optic, then close and reopen the deployment - everything persists exactly as before and the audit trail still records each action. ROLLBACK: revert this commit (safe to revert independently of .348 - it carries no behavior).
